body.title: Per-habit fire-rate counter — expose in habit_list MCP tool
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
132 habits have code_ref registered (counted via clan.memories WHERE memory_type='PROCEDURAL' AND metadata->>'code_ref' IS NOT NULL on 2026-04-27). Day-close-audit Step 12 verifies the code_ref points at a registered tool, but NOT whether the habit ever fires. 'Registered' ≠ 'live'. A habit could be perfectly registered with a working code_ref and never actually fire — invisible from current observability.

Work: add a fire counter (fired_count, last_fired_at) to PROC memory metadata. Increment in basal_ganglia.py at habit-fire path. Expose via mcp__igor__habit_list — sort by fire-rate, flag never-fired habits as 'cold'. Add a day-close-audit step that surfaces cold habits (registered but never fired).

Affected files:
- wild_igor/igor/cognition/basal_ganglia.py (or wherever the fire path lives — needs a quick read first to confirm)
- wild_igor/igor/tools/habit_tool.py or wherever habit_list is registered
- ~/.claude/skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md (new step or extend Step 12)

Design rules:
- postgres-only: ✓ (writes to clan.memories.metadata).
- oop-first: applicable — increment lives in BG fire method, not free function.
- docs-in-code: BG docstring should mention fire-rate side effect.
- no-new-memory-schemas: ✓ — uses existing metadata jsonb.
- test-plan-or-why-not: needs test coverage for: counter increments on fire, habit_list sort, cold-habit detection.

Scope boundary:
IN: counter + last_fired_at in metadata, sort/filter in habit_list, cold-habit audit step.
OUT: deleting cold habits (Akien-reviewed only), retraining BG threshold based on rate, fire-rate-driven salience changes.

Test plan:
- Unit: fire path increments fired_count, sets last_fired_at.
- Unit: habit_list returns habits sorted by fired_count desc; 'cold' filter selects fired_count==0.
- Integration: run a smoke test where a known habit fires, verify counter persists across restart.
- Audit step: grep test that running day-close-audit Step N produces 'cold habits: N' line.

Why now: lab/docs/capability_map.md §6.2 surfaced the gap (2026-04-27).
